pi_items.source: GCompris-qt/org.kde.gcompris.appdata.xml confirms Hexagon activity is in 'Games' category (https://raw.githubusercontent.com/gcompris/GCompris-qt/master/org.kde.gcompris.appdata.xml)
GCompris Hexagon activity documentation and source code - the game uses a hexagonal grid of cells

GCompris Hexagon activity - the strawberry is the hidden target object players must find. Confirmed in source code (https://raw.githubusercontent.com/gcompris/GCompris-qt/master/src/activities/hexagon/Hexagon.qml) and appdata.xml

pi_items.verified_value: red cell (part of color gradient feedback)
pi_items.source: GCompris Hexagon hexagon.js - red appears in the distance color gradient where red indicates closest distance (0-5 units)

pi_items.verified_value: yellow cell (part of color gradient feedback)
pi_items.source: GCompris Hexagon hexagon.js - yellow appears in the distance color gradient (5-10 units range)

GCompris Hexagon hexagon.js shows color feedback uses red, yellow, green, and blue gradient. No 'black' color is used in the distance feedback mechanism. Task description mentions 'Black/Blue means far' but implementation only uses blue.
Task specification in task.json and verified by setup_task.sh and export_result.sh which both reference this path

pi_items.verified_value: Red to yellow (0-5 units) = closest distance
pi_items.source: GCompris Hexagon hexagon.js getColor() function documentation

pi_items.verified_value: Yellow to green (5-10 units) = very close
pi_items.source: GCompris Hexagon hexagon.js getColor() function documentation

pi_items.verified_value: Blue means far (15+ units in the color gradient)
pi_items.source: GCompris Hexagon hexagon.js shows blue-to-red gradient for distances 15+. No black color is used.

privileged_info_summary: The hexagon_strawberry_search task uses the real GCompris educational software and the genuine Hexagon activity. The activity name 'Hexagon' is verified. However, the activity is located in the 'Games' category, not 'Puzzles' as claimed. The visual cues include hexagonal grid, strawberry, red cells, and yellow cells which are all verified. However, 'black cell' is not a valid visual cue - the color feedback system uses red, yellow, green, and blue in a heat map gradient where red indicates closest distance (0-5 units) and blue indicates farthest (15+ units). The task description incorrectly mentions 'Black/Blue means far' when only blue is used, not black. The expected output path '/home/ga/strawberry_found.png' is correctly specified.
